sections.paragraph:
The seventh letter of the alphabet called זַיִן, i.e. Syr. ܙܰܝܢܐܳ a weapon, which this letter resembles in form in all the more ancient alphabets. [“As a numeral it denotes 7.”]

In Arabic there are two letters which answer to this, which somewhat differ in pronunciation. ذ dh, and ز dz; as זָבַח ذَبَخَ to slaughter; זֶרַע زَرْعُ seed. When this letter corresponds to the former, it becomes in Aramean ד, when to the latter, ܙ is retained; thus ܕܒܰܚ, דְּבַה, to slaughter; ܙܪܰܥ זְרַע to sow, etc. Comp. the letter ד.

Also ذ and ز are interchanged amongst themselves; e.g. עָזַר, عَزَرَ and ܥܕܪܰ to help; גָּזַם, جذم and جزم to cut off.

ז is interchanged-(a) with ץ (ts) in זָעַק and צָעַק to cry out; עָלַז and עָלַץ to exult, to shout aloud; זָהָב gold; comp. צָהֹב tawny, yellow.-(b) with ס, שׂ as זוּר and סוּר to go away; עָלַז, עָלַס to exult; בָּזָה, Syr. ܒܣܳܐ to despise; אָסֹון damage, from אָסָה, اذى to hurt. [Also with ר, e.g. בָּרַק and בָּזָק. Thes.]
